claim.text: This repo generates adoption-safety evidence packs and Codex-ready smoke-test prompts before trusting a viral GitHub repo.

repository.name: oss-claimcheck
repository.description: Adoption-safety evidence pack generator for open-source maintainers

official_sources.title: README
quotes: `oss-claimcheck` produces a neutral evidence pack first, then generates suggested Codex-ready smoke-test prompts for follow-up verification.
`oss-claimcheck` is an adoption-safety evidence pack generator for open-source maintainers and AI builders.

It turns noisy social posts, product claims, and GitHub repository references into a structured Markdown/JSON evidence pack so maintainers can decide what is confirmed, unverified, or likely overstated before adopting a tool.

verification_checklist: security or destructive behavior is documented

confirmed: Official source quotes extracted from README
unverified: source text was provided by the user and still needs official-source verification

reasons: GitHub metadata available for maintenance checks
README gives maintainers a review surface
README can document setup and safety boundaries
repository identity was checked against GitHub metadata
no configured hype phrases detected in the claim text

evidence_score.note: Heuristic evidence score only; not a security audit.

follow_up_questions: What official docs or README sections support the claim?
Does the repo contain an installable package or only a demo?
Is the license present and compatible with OSS adoption?
What is confirmed by code versus marketing copy?
What should a maintainer smoke-test before adopting this tool?
